scope: national popular vote + abstentions for US presidential elections 2008-2024
purpose: Evaluation targets only. NOT for tuning the model.
primary_denominator: VEP (Voting-Eligible Population) per US Elections Project / UF Election Lab
fallback_denominator: Census CPS CVAP if VEP source unavailable for a year
convention_no_invented_numbers: Cells where exact value cannot be locally verified are null with needs_source: true.
source_hierarchy: US Elections Project / UF Election Lab VEP
Census CPS Voting and Registration Supplement (secondary)
CES/CCES (primary respondent-level survey microdata, separate file)

2008.d_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against FEC 2008 official PDF
2008.r_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against FEC 2008 official PDF
2008.vep_source: US Elections Project (Michael McDonald) / UF Election Lab
2008.notes: High-turnout cycle. D wins both popular vote and EC. Other includes Nader, Barr, McKinney.

2012.d_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against FEC 2012 official PDF
2012.r_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against FEC 2012 official PDF
2012.vep_source: US Elections Project / UF Election Lab

2016.d_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against FEC 2016 official
2016.r_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against FEC 2016 official
2016.other_votes_note: 2016 had elevated third-party share (Johnson L, Stein G, McMullin). Derive as Total − D − R after exact total verified.
2016.vep_source: US Elections Project / UF Election Lab
2016.notes: D wins popular vote, R wins EC. CCES 2016 microdata available locally for backtest.

2020.d_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against FEC 2020 official PDF
2020.r_votes_verification: FEC search snippet — verify against FEC 2020 official PDF
2020.total_presidential_votes_verification: approximate (158.4 million ballots) per UF Election Lab search snippet; verify exact via FEC
2020.vep_source: US Elections Project / UF Election Lab
2020.turnout_vep_discrepancy: Conflicting search snippets cite 66.1% AND 66.6%. Reconcile against UF Election Lab tables before use.
2020.notes: Highest VEP turnout in over a century. Conflicting turnout-rate citations require explicit reconciliation.

2024.d_votes_verification: FEC PDF directly verified — page 3 of Official 2024 Presidential General Election Results

other_votes_breakdown_partial.note: Partial breakdown from FEC PDF pages 2-6; not exhaustive

2024.total_presidential_votes_verification: FEC PDF directly verified — page 6 'Total Votes' Total row
2024.vep_source: US Elections Project / UF Election Lab
2024.turnout_vep_discrepancy: Conflicting search snippets cite 64.3% AND 62.5%. Reconcile against UF Election Lab 2024 turnout page before use.
2024.notes: First R popular-vote win since 2004. Notable third-party: Kennedy 756,393, De La Cruz 166,175, Stein, Oliver, etc. Total Other 2,918,109 (1.88% of total).

verified_vs_needs_source_summary.highest_confidence_year: 2024 (FEC PDF directly verified for D, R, Other, Total)
verified_vs_needs_source_summary.largest_gap: VEP for every year — UF Election Lab data tables not locally verified; turnout-rate citations inconsistent for 2020 and 2024

comparison_anchors.note: These four 2024 anchors are the strongest verified comparison points in this benchmark file. Other years require additional source pulls before exact comparisons can be made.
